name: Hubab ibn Amir
title: Follower of Muslim
shortDescription: A Shia from Kufa who secretly left the city after Muslim ibn Aqeel's martyrdom and joined the Imam in Karbala.
fullStory:
Hubab ibn Amir al-Ta'i was a sincere and brave Shia in Kufa. He pledged allegiance to Muslim ibn Aqeel upon his arrival in Kufa and was among his supporters.

After the Kufans' betrayal and the oppressed martyrdom of Muslim ibn Aqeel, the situation in Kufa became highly strict and secure. However, Hubab did not give in to fear and, instead of staying in the city, secretly left Kufa.

He traveled until he joined the Master of Martyrs' (AS) caravan along the way (or in Karbala itself). On Ashura, like other self-sacrificing companions, he stood against the enemy's attack.

Hubab ibn Amir was martyred in Yazid's army's first attack (morning shower of arrows) with a body full of arrows and wounds, receiving greetings from the infallible Imam in Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya.
quote: (Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya:) Peace be upon Hubab ibn Amir...
